real_architecture_summary: run_all builds a pipeline plan, validates the static registry, bootstraps a run manifest, then executes stages through pipeline_execution and execution_engine. Success is largely subprocess-exit and stage-manifest-schema based; declared artifact contracts are not reconciled against actual produced files before run success is granted.
top_invariants: Successful stages must prove required outputs exist, not just return 0 and write a manifest shell.
Certification mode must turn on strict read isolation and required stage-manifest enforcement, not merely record those expectations in run_manifest.json.
Run-manifest success must be derived from verified stage artifacts, not terminal paperwork alone.

findings.title: Certification mode records strict artifact-safety flags but never enables the environment gates that enforce them
findings.evidence: run_all_bootstrap writes strict_run_scoped_reads and require_stage_manifests into the run manifest, but execution gates in project/io/utils.py and project/pipelines/execution_engine.py only read BACKTEST_STRICT_RUN_SCOPED_READS and BACKTEST_REQUIRE_STAGE_MANIFEST, which run_all never exports.
findings.why_it_matters: Certification mode advertises fail-closed artifact isolation while still allowing the default fallback behavior.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Run project.pipelines.run_all in certification mode and inspect both run_manifest.json and the run_all environment-handling code.
findings.suggested_remediation: Export the enforcement env vars from run_all in certification mode and add an execution-path test that proves the stricter behavior is active.

findings.title: build_features can exit success with no inputs, no outputs, and no per-symbol results
findings.evidence: build_features initializes empty inputs/outputs, continues when cleaned bars are absent, never appends written outputs to the manifest, and still finalizes success. The manifest schema only checks that outputs is a list, not that success manifests declare artifacts.
findings.why_it_matters: A green feature stage can represent zero produced artifacts, which is a false-green path and breaks cache/reconciliation logic.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Run project.pipelines.features.build_features against an empty temporary BACKTEST_DATA_ROOT and inspect the emitted stage manifest.
findings.suggested_remediation: Make zero-produced-symbol runs fail or warn, and require successful manifests to declare existing outputs.

findings.title: ingest_binance_um_ohlcv converts total fetch failure into a green stage with only missing-archive stats
findings.evidence: async_main() classifies failed/not_found monthly fetches as missing_archives, raises nothing when all months fail, and main() still finalizes success with empty outputs.
findings.why_it_matters: The root raw-data producer can go green when no requested market data was actually ingested.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Monkeypatch _process_month to always return failed/not_found and inspect async_main() and main() behavior.
findings.suggested_remediation: Treat coverage failure as terminal when required partitions are missing and record actual written partitions into manifest outputs.

findings.title: Funding ingestion records missing coverage but still returns success, allowing partial or empty funding artifacts to masquerade as complete
findings.evidence: ingest_binance_um_funding computes missing_after_all and missing_count, but finalizes success regardless; build_features treats funding as optional, and the registry models funding as optional input for build_features_*.
findings.why_it_matters: A run can appear funding-aware while silently dropping funding-derived signal content.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Trace missing_after_all handling in ingest_binance_um_funding.py and build_features funding-read behavior.
findings.suggested_remediation: Differentiate complete success from partial coverage and add configurable enforcement when funding-derived features are expected.

findings.title: Run-manifest reconciliation upgrades failed runs to success without checking declared outputs or artifact existence
findings.evidence: reconcile_run_manifest_from_stage_manifests() promotes the run to success when all planned stage manifests are terminal, without validating outputs, hashes, or on-disk existence. The tests explicitly encode warning/success-only reconciliation.
findings.why_it_matters: Manual or stale manifests can flip a failed run to success even when artifacts are missing or empty.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Create terminal stage manifests with empty/missing outputs for a failed run and call reconcile_run_manifest_from_stage_manifests().
findings.suggested_remediation: Require reconciliation to validate declared outputs against disk and, ideally, against the artifact contract before promoting the run.

test_coverage_assessment: Static contract tests exist, but runtime artifact truth is weakly protected. There are no tests requiring successful stages to declare non-empty outputs or certification mode to actually enable strict isolation.
docs_specs_code_contradictions: run_all_bootstrap records certification guarantees in run_manifest.json, but runtime enforcement only reads env vars that run_all never sets.
pipeline_registry presents artifact contracts as authoritative, but runtime success does not reconcile against them.
suggested_regression_tests: A certification-mode execution test proving strict env gates are active.
A success-manifest test that requires non-empty, existing outputs for build_features.
A reconciliation test that keeps the run failed when stage outputs are missing.
open_questions_or_unknowns: Whether any external tooling intentionally promotes warning-only stage sets despite missing artifacts.
